Title: Elderly man takes wife on trishaw ride for glimpse of PM
Article:
BATU PAHAT: The scorching heat did not deter an 86-year-old man from pedalling his trishaw for nearly a kilometre to fulfil his wife's wish of seeing Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim up close during his breakfast stop at a coffee shop in Senggarang here today.
Abdul Razak Pelangga, 87, said he decided to take his wife, Jamilah Samsudin, 64, on the trishaw from their home in Kampung Parit Bugis to Kedai Kopi Hailam Sri Medan after learning yesterday that the prime minister would be visiting the small town.
The imam of Masjid Raudhatul Jannah Parit Bugis said although he could have driven a car or ridden a motorcycle, he opted for the trishaw to avoid the hassle of finding a parking space.
"Many years ago, before he became prime minister, he came to perform prayers at our mosque. I led the congregational prayers, and afterwards we shook hands and exchanged greetings. He was very friendly, and I shared that experience with my wife.
"When I found out he would be in the area, I was determined to bring my wife to meet the prime minister. We are both happy today. Although we could not meet him face to face because of the large crowd, seeing him from a close distance was enough. All this while we had only watched him on television," he told Bernama today.
Earlier, Anwar spent about 50 minutes at the coffee shop while mingling with local residents during the PMX Meet-and-Greet Programme for the Senggarang state constituency in conjunction with the Johor state election.
Also present was Pakatan Harapan communications director and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
Meanwhile, Abdul Razak said he was excited to fulfil his civic duty on July 11, which coincides with his 87th birthday.
"I've voted many times before, but this one feels a little more special because I get to fulfil my civic duty on my birthday," he said.
Polling for the Johor state election will be held on July 11, while early voting is scheduled for July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH grades Johor seats for election strategy
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) is classifying all 56 Johor seats based on priority levels as part of its election strategy, according to current ground support trends.
PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the "grading" system allows the coalition to focus resources on key constituencies with different voter profiles and challenges.
"We assess the areas from the start of our campaign. Puteri Wangsa, for example, is not the same as Johor Lama, and Larkin is different from Endau.
"We have grouped constituencies according to priority levels," he told reporters after a breakfast session with the Chinese community.
Also present was PH Puteri Wangsa candidate Dr Maszlee Malik.
Saifuddin, who is also PKR election director, said PH's improving support on the ground was driven by effective campaign strategy as well as political developments involving rival parties.
He cited Pas' decision to contest only 11 seats and its call for supporters to back Barisan Nasional in other constituencies as one factor influencing voter dynamics in PH's favour.
In contrast, he said PH had taken a more transparent approach by clearly announcing its seat distribution and offering a "reasonable and deliverable" manifesto.
Saifuddin also said that former Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi's attendance at a recent ceramah with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim in Felda Ulu Tiram is a positive boost to PH's momentum.
He added that Maszlee is a capable candidate and an important asset to PH if the coalition is given the mandate to form the state government.
PH is contesting all 56 Johor state seats, with PKR fielding 20 candidates, Amanah 19, and DAP 17.
A total of 172 candidates are contesting in the Johor election, with polling set for July 11 and early voting on July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt to monitor Budi Diesel based on data, feedback - Amir Hamzah
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The federal government will continuously assess the implementation of the Budi Diesel initiative based on data collected on the ground, including usage patterns and feedback from stakeholders, said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan.
Responding to a question on whether there is a review period to reassess the quota structure after five days of rollout on July 1, he said the approach remains flexible and data-driven, with adjustments made based on actual usage patterns.
"We will always look at what the data tells us. For example, under Budi95, the original quota was 300 litres, but it was later revised to 200 litres based on usage patterns.
"The adjustment was not made to cut quotas, but based on actual data. It showed that more than 94 to 95 per cent of users were below 300 litres, while those consistently using more than 200 litres accounted for less than one per cent.
"The government always refines policies based on data," he told a press conference here today.
Amir Hamzah said the Budi Diesel programme has only been implemented for about five days in Sabah and Sarawak, with initial focus on ensuring system stability and access for eligible users.
He said all petrol stations have been operating the system without disruption so far, and users have been able to make purchases as usual.
He added that the government will continue to monitor implementation data while also considering feedback on specific ground realities, with ministries and agencies working together to ensure proper assessment and targeted intervention.
Amir Hamzah said any overall reassessment of the quota structure would take time as monitoring of rollout data continues.
However, he said individual issues such as vehicle ownership concerns raised through feedback are already being reviewed, with government teams deployed on the ground to engage stakeholders.
He said the mechanism must remain flexible, noting that while MyKad is used for individual users, alternative arrangements such as fleet cards could be considered for service providers, including those in rural areas, to ensure subsidies are targeted appropriately.
On the Budi Diesel enquiry counter initiative at petrol stations, he said it is important to improve public understanding through collaboration with oil companies and station operators, including providing assistance on eligibility checks via QR codes and online applications.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two schoolboys arrested after ramming cop at Seremban roadblock
Article:
SEREMBAN: Police arrested two schoolboys after they rammed a policeman while trying to escape a roadblock during Op Samseng Jalanan at Taman Panchor Jaya yesterday.
Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the teenagers, aged 14 and 15, were riding a Honda EX5 motorcycle and were detained at 6.45pm.
"The duo acted aggressively by trying to escape and rammed a policeman, causing bruises to his head, right ankle, right hand, and waist.
"The victim received treatment at the Tuanku Ja'afar Hospital emergency ward and was allowed to return home after an examination revealed no serious injuries," he said in a statement today.
Alzafny said urine tests on both suspects returned negative, and the 14-year-old rider has been remanded for two days until tomorrow.
The case is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, as well as Section 186 and Section 332 of the Penal Code.
He added that 49 summonses were issued for various offences during the six-hour operation, including riding without a helmet, riding without a licence, and illegal exhaust modifications.
Twelve motorcycles were also seized.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mother, two children arrested for posing as welfare officers, duping elderly woman
Article:
IPOH: Police have arrested a mother and her two children for allegedly cheating a 67-year-old woman out of jewellery worth about RM8,000 by posing as welfare officers.
Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the victim was waiting for a medical check-up at a hospital in Teluk Intan on June 29 when she was approached by a woman claiming to be a welfare officer.
Alwi said the suspect allegedly offered the victim RM2,000 a month in welfare assistance before asking her to hand over the jewellery she was wearing, purportedly as part of the application process.
"Believing the offer to be genuine, the victim handed over a gold necklace, a pair of gold earrings with a short chain, and three gold rings.
"The suspect left with the jewellery and never returned, prompting the victim to realise she had been cheated.
"The victim suffered losses estimated at RM8,000 before lodging a police report," he said in a statement today.
He said police arrested the family at an apartment in Semenyih, Selangor, at 4.10pm on Friday following intelligence gathered during the investigation.
He said the suspects, comprising two women and a man aged between 22 and 50, are believed to be a mother and her two children.
"Police also seized clothing believed to have been worn by the suspects during the commission of the offence.
"We recovered the victim's jewellery after it was traced to a pawnshop in Teluk Intan, where it had allegedly been sold by one of the suspects," he said.
He added that they have been remanded for four days, until tomorrow, to assist with the investigation.
The case is being investigated under Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating, which carries a jail term of between one and 10 years, whipping, and a fine upon conviction.
He advised the public not to be easily deceived by individuals claiming to offer welfare assistance in exchange for jewellery or cash.
He urged those with information on similar scams to contact the nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Certain political parties uneasy over my firm anti-corruption stance
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim claimed that certain political parties were joining forces to bring him down because his administration has taken a firm stance against corruption and abuse of power.
The Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman said the Madani government's uncompromising approach towards those who steal public funds or abuse their positions for personal gain had made certain quarters uneasy.
"That is why, as you can see now, all these parties are joining forces to bring me down. Why? Because they know we are strict. They know we safeguard everything," he said while speaking at the PMX Meet-and-Greet programme for the Senggarang state constituency in conjunction with the Johor state election here today.
Also present was PH Communications director and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
Anwar said he was not intimidated by the political pressure, as his administration's guiding principle was clear -  to ensure that leaders did not abuse their power to acquire land, secure projects, obtain shares or accumulate personal wealth.
He said that despite making numerous visits to Johor as prime minister, he had never taken any land, projects or shares in the state.
"I have come to Johor dozens of times. Did I take any land? No. Did I take any projects? No. Did I take any shares? No. We must instil this principle in our elected representatives. If we want a prosperous state, we must uphold integrity," he said.
In this regard, Anwar called on Johor voters to give PH the opportunity to govern the state in order to bring about more comprehensive change, particularly in improving public welfare and ensuring clean governance.
He said Johor should have a state government that is aligned with the federal government so that development initiatives and assistance programmes for the people could be implemented more effectively.
The prime minister said that although Johor had received billions of ringgit worth of development projects, there were still fundamental issues affecting the people that required attention, including housing, roads, mosques, surau, places of worship, and assistance for those in need.
"Development is progressing, with projects worth billions of ringgit, yet houses remain unaffordable for ordinary people. That is the responsibility of the state government. It has not done enough. Johor is a wealthy state.
"We do not want only a small group to become rich while the majority of the people remain poor," he said.
Anwar also defended PH's cooperation with DAP, stressing that throughout his three-and-a-half years as prime minister, DAP representatives in the Cabinet had never opposed programmes benefiting the Malays or Islam.
"I have been prime minister for three and a half years, and not once have DAP ministers in the Cabinet opposed any programme for the Malays or Islam. You can check the record," he said.
Meanwhile, Anwar said he was deeply touched by the enthusiastic turnout of PH supporters in Senggarang, describing it as extraordinary, as many had waited under the hot sun to attend the programme.
"The spirit in Senggarang is extraordinary. Even under the scorching sun, the people were willing to wait. Rarely have I seen such enthusiasm at one of my ceramah despite the heat," he said, while urging voters in Senggarang, Rengit and Penggaram to give their full support to PH candidates in the Johor state election.
The Johor state election has drawn 172 candidates contesting 56 state seats. Polling is scheduled for July 11, while early voting will be held on Tuesday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Muda says PH out of touch for omitting flood issues from manifesto
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Muda has questioned Pakatan Harapan's (PH) manifesto, claiming it makes no reference to flooding, climate change or environmental issues affecting the state.
Its Puteri Wangsa candidate Rashifa Aljunied said the omission reflected a lack of understanding of Johor voters' priorities, particularly as floods have become more frequent and severe.
"If the PH manifesto truly understood the voice of the people of Johor, they would realise that floods have caused significant losses to residents.
"Yet, there is not a single mention of floods, climate change or the environment in the manifesto," she said in a statement today.
Rashifa said flooding remained a persistent issue in Puteri Wangsa, occurring during the monsoon season as well as during periods of heavy rain and high tides.
She added that more than 8,000 flood victims had been given help in the area, while the number of flood hotspots in Johor had reportedly increased from about 745 to 874.
She also noted that Puteri Wangsa incumbent Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz, who sits on the state committee for climate change and disaster management, had consistently raised flood mitigation issues at the policy level.
Rashifa said the absence of environmental and flood-related commitments in the PH manifesto suggested the coalition was not fully attuned to local needs and was unprepared to govern the state with a focus on public welfare.
On Friday, PH election director Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari launched the coalition's
'Johor for All' election manifesto,
targeting a 'Shenzhen of Southeast Asia' economic transformation with 10 key pledges ahead of the July 11 Johor election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Defence Ministry will not allow KDA talks to delay RM1.06b NSM claim
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace AS (KDA) has requested talks with the Defence Ministry to resolve a RM1.06 billion claim following the cancellation of the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system for the Royal Malaysian Navy.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, however, stressed that the ministry will not allow the talks to be used as a tactic to delay the claims settlement process.
He said the ministry remains vigilant against any attempts that could disrupt the process.
"The company, KDA, has requested to discuss with us, but while we say we can talk, we do not want it to be used as a tactic to delay the claim made previously.
"We are always aware and vigilant if such tactics are used so that we do not fall into a trap," he said after attending the Community Religious Education Programme  at FELDA Air Tawar 2 here today.
Khaled added that an evaluation team comprising navy officers has been established to identify the best alternative missile system from four countries to replace the Norway-made NSM.
He said the four options involve Turkiye, South Korea, and two European nations.
Previously, the Norwegian government reportedly revoked the export licence for the NSM anti-ship missiles to Malaysia, including the related launcher systems, citing national security reasons.
According to the Kongsberg Defence and Aerospace AS (KDA) website, the NSM procurement was inked between the RMN and KDA in April 2018, with a contract value of €124 million (RM571.9 million) to equip six new Littoral Combat Ships (LCS).
Following the cancellation, Malaysia filed a RM1.06 billion claim against KDA, which includes a direct claim of €129.86 million (about RM604 million) for payments already made by the government.
It also covers an indirect claim of €96.26 million (about RM448.1 million) involving cost implications and side effects resulting from the failure to supply the system.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar takes swipe at Onn Hafiz over federal-state meeting attendance
Article:
SEGAMAT: Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has taken a swipe at caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, questioning his absence from federal–state coordination meetings.
Anwar said cooperation between Putrajaya and state governments is essential for the benefit of the people.
The prime minister also said he would convene a meeting with all menteri besar, chief ministers, and the Sarawak Premier within the next month to discuss development issues, including matters involving Felda settlers.
However, he suggested that Johor's representative had not been attending such meetings.
"Within a month, I will chair a meeting with all menteri besar, chief ministers, and the Sarawak Premier.
"I hope all the menteri besar will attend. (In the previous meeting) the Kelantan Menteri Besar came. The Kedah Menteri Besar, even though he is very fierce, also came.
"As for Johor… I don't know," he said, drawing laughter from the crowd.
Anwar quickly softened his remarks, saying cooperation should remain the priority regardless of political differences.
"Come to the meeting. We must cooperate. I am not asking for Johor's land, I am not asking for Johor's timber, and I am not asking for Johor's projects.
"We need cooperation because it is the people who benefit."
On June 23, Anwar chaired the 149th Meeting of Menteri Besar and Chief Ministers, which focused on strengthening and revitalising the national economy following global uncertainty arising from conflicts in the Middle East.
However, Onn Hafiz was absent from the meeting.
Prior to that, Onn Hafiz had claimed he was prevented from entering a Felda settlement to present land titles to settlers during the 2026 Johor Felda Settlers' Day celebration.
As a result, the event, which was originally scheduled to take place at Felda Ulu Belitong, Kluang, on June 20 and 21, was moved to Dewan Dato' Onn in Taman Sri Lambak, Kluang.
On the same matter, Anwar said a good working relationship between the federal and state governments was especially important in resolving issues involving Felda, affordable housing, and infrastructure, many of which require cooperation from both administrations.
He also said the federal government had continued approving major projects in Johor, including the Johor Bahru Autonomous Rapid Transit (ART) system, the revival of Forest City, and the Maharani Port project.
Anwar maintained that political rivalry should not stand in the way of development.
"When the federal and state governments respect each other and work together, the people are the ones who benefit."
The Johor election will be held on July 11, with early voting taking place on Tuesday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Synthetic drugs account for 83pct of drug abuse cases nationwide
Article:
LABUAN: Synthetic drugs account for 83 per cent of drug abuse cases recorded in the country, prompting the National Anti-Drugs Agency (Nada) to closely monitor the emerging trend involving fentanyl, its director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said.
He said methamphetamine remained the most commonly abused synthetic drug, while the remaining 17 per cent of cases involved traditional drugs such as cannabis and heroin.
"The use of synthetic drugs is worrying as we are seeing various types being abused, with methamphetamine being the highest.
"The agency is also monitoring the new trend involving fentanyl, and the agency is equipped with urine test strips to detect certain substances," he told reporters after launching the Jelajah Aspirasi Bebas Dadah and Kampung Aspirasi Bebas Dadah programme at Dataran Kampung Belukut here today.
He said the test strips available to Nada could detect several substances, including amphetamine-type stimulants, or methamphetamine, fentanyl, AB-PINACA, cocaine and ketamine.
Ruslin said fentanyl is a highly potent synthetic opioid that is 50 to 100 times stronger than morphine, and from recent seizures involving such drug, there was a need for the agency to strengthen its monitoring to identify the presence and pattern of new synthetic drug abuse in the country.
He said urine tests would also be conducted again on those undergoing rehabilitation to identify the specific types of drugs they had taken.
"We do not want to only know that a person is involved in drug abuse, but also to identify what type of drug is being abused so that intervention and rehabilitation can be more targeted."
Ruslin said Nada was also concerned over the abuse of drugs through electronic cigarettes and vape devices, which posed a growing challenge in detection and prevention efforts.
On the increase in drug cases in states located near border areas, he said efforts must not only focus on enforcement and supply control, but also on reducing demand.
Meanwhile, he said Labuan currently had 268 drug addicts undergoing Nada's rehabilitation process, with males accounting for 91.6 per cent and women making up the remaining percentage.
For 2026, amphetamine-type stimulants accounted for 98.5 per cent of drug abuse cases in Labuan, while the remaining cases involved traditional drugs, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar summons Felda management tomorrow to resolve settlers' long-standing issues
Article:
SEGAMAT: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will summon the Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) management to the Prime Minister's Office tomorrow.
This is part of efforts to resolve long-standing issues affecting settlers, particularly those involving second-generation land ownership and housing.
The Pakatan Harapan chairman said his administration had already adopted a policy to ensure that second-generation Felda settlers are guaranteed land for housing.
He said the government was committed to addressing longstanding Felda-related issues as part of ongoing efforts to improve the welfare of settlers.
"I hope that for this Johor election, we can bring about change. I am responsible for Felda,I cannot simply ignore it.
"Although I have visited Felda areas in Pahang, Perak and Terengganu, this is my first time here. Insya-Allah, I have already instructed the relevant and members of parliament that over the next two weeks this will be our focus.
"Tomorrow I will call the Felda management to the Prime Minister's Office. On Tuesday we have National Settlers' Day.
"Within two weeks, the minister and members of parliament will meet settler representatives to discuss what has been decided and what can be implemented,"he said during his speech at the Jelajah Harapan Felda programme with PMX here today.
Anwar said the government had already made a policy decision at the end of last year to secure land sites for Felda's second generation, although he could not announce further details during the election campaign because of regulations.
He said many longstanding issues remained unresolved, including abandoned housing projects and housing areas without basic infrastructure such as roads, drainage, electricity and water supply.
"The policy decision has already been made to find a solution and guarantee land sites for the second generation of Felda settlers. That is the policy."
Anwar who is also finance minister acknowledged that many Felda communities continued to face unresolved issues, including land ownership, abandoned housing projects and housing sites without basic infrastructure.
"There are housing projects that have stalled, while others cannot be developed because there is no electricity, water supply, drainage and other basic infrastructure.
"I cannot announce more because of election regulations, but I want to tell you that the government has already made this policy decision since the end of last year."
He said the federal government was committed to resolving the issue but stressed that cooperation from state governments was equally important because land matters fell under state jurisdiction.
"Felda is under the federal government, but the land belongs to the state. Infrastructure also requires cooperation with the state government.
"When the federal and state governments work together respectfully, it is the people who benefit."
Anwar also announced that this year's Hari Peneroka Felda celebration would be held in Temerloh, Pahang, instead of Kuala Lumpur, saying the event should be brought closer to settlers rather than organised in the capital.
He said future celebrations could be rotated to other states, including Johor, provided there was cooperation from the state government.
The prime minister also used his speech to criticise previous Felda management, blaming poor governance and abuse of trust for the agency's financial troubles.
"Felda was established to help landless Malays, but when people stayed in power for too long, some became arrogant and sought to profit from the hard work of the people below.
"They shouted about protecting the Malays, but when entrusted with responsibility, it was Malay assets that were looted. Felda suffered billions of ringgit in losses because of poor management, not because the settlers failed."
Anwar said that since becoming finance minister in 2023, the federal government had been paying nearly RM1 billion annually to service Felda's legacy debts.
He urged voters not to be swayed by rhetoric but to judge leaders based on integrity and their ability to safeguard public funds.
"Being a Malay leader means protecting the interests of the Malays honestly, not stealing from them.
"I want to prove that when entrusted with power, we can govern cleanly, not steal public money, and serve the people properly."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC denies man in viral social media post is its officer
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has denied claims that a man featured in a viral social media post is one of its officers.
The commission said that such acts are unauthorised, unlawful and could undermine the integrity of the institution.
"The public is advised to exercise caution when dealing with those claiming to be MACC officers.
"Verify the identity of MACC officers through official channels before providing any information or cooperation.
"Report any suspected impersonation incidents to the MACC immediately.
"The MACC emphasises that impersonating its officers is a criminal offence," it said in a statement.
The MACC said offenders can be prosecuted under Section 170 of the Penal Code for impersonating a public servant and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act for improper use of network facilities.
A woman on Threads had earlier today uploaded a photograph of an MACC officer identification card bearing the name Amirul Haikal Hassan.
Based on screenshots of conversations uploaded by the woman, the man also claimed to be a divorcee with one child.
Members of the public can verify information through the MACC's official channels, including its website, social media accounts on Facebook, Instagram, TikTok and X, as well as its toll-free hotline at 1-800-88-6000.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Main puteri, menora return to public stage as Kelantan tourism assets
Article:
PASIR PUTEH: Traditional performing arts once banned from public viewing in Kelantan are making a comeback through a cultural tourism initiative led by the National Department for Culture and Arts.
Its director-general, Mohd Amran Mohd Haris, said the revived performances include main puteri, menora and makyong.
The art forms were barred from public performances in the 1990s after being deemed to contain superstitious elements and to be inconsistent with Islamic teachings.
"Reviving these performances is not only about preserving Kelantan's cultural identity, but also about creating tourism products that can attract local and international visitors," he said after officiating the 2026 Kelantan Folk Arts Festival at the Tok Bali Tourism Jetty.
Also present were Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud and Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry secretary-general Datuk Shaharuddin Abu Sohot.
Amran said public performances would be conducted in accordance with the state's Entertainment Enactment and the guidelines set by the Kelantan government.
He added that every state has unique cultural traditions that should be introduced to the world through cultural tourism while also being passed down to younger generations to prevent them from disappearing.
He noted that eight Malaysian cultural elements have been recognised by the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organisation (Unesco) as Intangible Cultural Heritage: Dondang Sayang, Makyong, Silat, Pantun, the Wangkang Ceremony, Songket Weaving, the Memondok (Ponggal) Ceremony, and the Mek Mulung theatre performance.
Amran said the department is now seeking similar Unesco recognition for dikir barat.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kedah Sultan urges review of Penang lease payment, respect for state's constitutional rights
Article:
ALOR STAR: The Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, has called on the federal government to review the lease payment for Penang based on current rates, saying the existing payment no longer reflects the spirit and original intent of the agreement.
His Royal Highness said the lease payment received by the Kedah government was based on a long-standing agreement but was now "far lower than what it should be".
"I wish to remind all parties that the rights and sovereignty of the state of Kedah, as enshrined in the Federal Constitution of Malaysia, must be respected by all," he said at the 2026 Kedah State Awards and Honours Investiture Ceremony at Istana Anak Bukit today.
The ruler also called on the federal government to cooperate in ensuring there are no administrative obstacles to the state's development, including in the management of rare earth elements (REE).
His Highness said Kedah's rights and authority to manage and explore its natural resources must be respected and facilitated.
"It would be a great loss if this matter fails to be implemented simply because of differences in political views and understanding, thereby disregarding the rights and needs of the state," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three sons of Iran's slain leader Khamenei appear at funeral, not his successor
Article:
TEHERAN: Three sons of slain Iranian leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei prayed beside his coffin and those of four other family members on Sunday, but Mojtaba, the son who has succeeded him as Iran's supreme leader, did not make an appearance.
State TV showed Mostafa, Meysam and Masoud Khamenei praying behind the coffins laid out in the vast courtyard of Tehran's Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla, a sprawling religious complex.
In a show of public devotion to the theocratic state and revolutionary zeal, the Islamic Republic is staging a week of mass funeral processions for Khamenei, including taking his remains to Shi'ite religious sites in neighbouring Iraq.
After a day lying in state indoors for senior Iranian leaders and foreign officials to visit, Khamenei's coffin was displayed outdoors on Saturday under glass, along with those of his daughter, son-in-law, daughter-in-law and 14-month-old granddaughter.
There has still been no public sighting or image released of Mojtaba, said to have been injured in the attack that killed his father and the other family members on February 28, when Israel and the US bombed Iranian targets at the start of the war.
Mojtaba Khamenei's face was disfigured, and he suffered a significant injury to one or both legs, people close to his inner circle told Reuters.
A ceasefire has suspended the four-month-old war under an agreement with Washington that Iran's authorities say will ultimately bring huge economic benefits, in line with what they describe as a victory over a superpower.
US President Donald Trump told the Axios news website that peace talks had been paused for a week for the events surrounding the funeral.
On Sunday, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf prayed behind the coffins.
Masoud Khamenei was seen crying and wiping his tears with a keffiyeh – the chequered scarf that is a symbol in Iran of militant revolutionary ideals and solidarity with Palestinians – as an imam recited funeral prayers.
Crowds of Iranians, many weeping and some beating their chests, have thronged the Mosalla, including overnight.
The Iranian metro railway network said it had clocked 7 million trips from late on Saturday to Sunday morning as people flocked to the centre.
After what authorities are billing as a massive procession in central Tehran on Monday, the remains will be taken to the seminary city of Qom, the centre of Iran's Shi'ite hierarchy, for ceremonies on Tuesday.
From there, the body will be flown to Iraq for ceremonies in the Shi'ite holy ⁠shrine cities of ​Najaf and Kerbala on Wednesday.
It will return to Iran on Thursday for another ​procession in Mashhad, to be buried near the tomb of another of the medieval Shi'ite imams.
Authorities plan to mobilise millions of people for big processions over the coming days, offering transport, food and lodging. — REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MBSA speeds up 200m walkway near Stadium Shah Alam LRT3 station
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A section of the 200m pedestrian walkway linking the LRT3 Stadium Shah Alam station to a nearby traffic light junction is expected to be completed tomorrow, following public complaints over the lack of safe pedestrian access.
The project was carried out by the Shah Alam City Council (MBSA) as an immediate response after a site visit by Selangor Local Government and Tourism Committee chairman Ng Suee Lim and Kota Anggerik assemblyman Mohd Najwan Halimi on Wednesday.
Najwan, who is also the state Youth, Sports and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman, said in a Threads post that the 200m stretch along Persiaran Sukan, Section 13, is on track for completion tomorrow as an initial phase of the wider pedestrian link.
He said the overall walkway project also includes an extension along Persiaran Akuatik, with construction on that section scheduled to begin on Tuesday.
He thanked pedestrian advocacy group @kakijalankaki and members of the public for highlighting the issue.
MBSA said on Wednesday it would expedite construction of the walkway after concerns over pedestrian access at newly opened LRT3 stations went viral on social media.
The council said it had taken note of feedback on pedestrian facilities at several stations, including Dato' Menteri, Stadium Shah Alam, Section 7 and Kerjaya, which began operations on Monday.
MBSA said it remained committed to providing safe, accessible public infrastructure.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCMC to expand MOCN to improve internet connectivity in poor coverage areas
Article:
BATU PAHAT: The Communications Ministry, through the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), plans to expand the implementation of the Multi-Operator Core Network (MOCN) nationwide to improve connectivity in areas with limited internet coverage.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the initiative would enable telecommunications companies to collaborate, particularly in extending coverage to rural, remote and underserved areas with weak signal reception.
He said that under the MOCN initiative, telecommunications companies with existing infrastructure in a particular area could share their network with other operators, allowing mobile signals to be transmitted and received more comprehensively.
"At present, the MOCN initiative has been implemented at five locations - two telecommunications towers at Bukit Putus in Negri Sembilan, and one each at Prima Gambang in Pahang, the Kota Seri Langat Toll Plaza on the West Coast Expressway (WCE) in Banting, Selangor, and Tanjung Asam in Penang.
"In Johor, we are currently identifying several areas for implementation. Based on assessments and studies carried out by the MCMC, areas with limited 4G or 5G coverage are mainly located in rural parts of the state," he told reporters after the Communications Ministry's Ziarah Kasih programme at Kampung Parit Maimon here today.
Fahmi said one of the main factors contributing to poor mobile signal reception was that many homes were located in villages and rural areas surrounded or shielded by oil palm trees.
"Johor remains a priority for the ministry and the MCMC as we work towards achieving 100 per cent internet coverage in populated areas," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN's Onn Hafiz courts early security forces voters, seeks continued mandate [WATCH]
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Caretaker Johor menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has appealed to security forces in the state to support Barisan Nasional ahead of early voting for the polls on July 7.
In a message on Facebook, he said the military, police and other security agencies played a crucial role in maintaining peace, stability and security in the state and the country.
"I would like to record my highest appreciation and gratitude for your service, sacrifice and dedication in safeguarding the security and stability of Johor and the nation.
"Leading and administering Johor is not an easy responsibility. The trust given by the people must be carried with full accountability.
"As such, I humbly appeal to all of you. Give your trust and an opportunity to BN candidates so that we can continue the work that has been started and take Johor further for the well-being of all Bangsa Johor," he said.
He added that BN would continue to work hard if given another mandate, pledging to serve the people to the best of its ability.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 12 undocumented migrants fined for illegal entry, overstaying in Kelantan
Article:
PASIR MAS: Three women were among 12 undocumented migrants who were charged at the Sessions Court here today for illegal entry, overstaying and misuse of passes.
The 12, aged between 21 and 57, pleaded guilty to the offences before judge Zulkifli Abdullah.
The three women, comprising two Indonesians and one Thai national, were fined RM1,000, RM6,000 and RM10,000 respectively, in default of between one and three months' imprisonment, for offences involving illegal entry, overstaying and misuse of passes.
The remaining nine accused, all men from Myanmar, Pakistan, Bangladesh and Indonesia, were fined between RM1,000 and RM10,000 each, in default of up to three months' jail.
Seven were charged with overstaying under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63.
Four others were charged under Section 6(1)(c) of the same Act for entering and remaining in Malaysia without valid travel documents, while another was charged under Regulation 39(b) of the Immigration Regulations 1963 for misusing her pass.
Some of the accused, through a court interpreter, appealed for leniency, citing financial hardship and family responsibilities in their home countries.
Siti Khaidijah Khairuddin prosecuted, while all the accused were unrepresented.
The Immigration Department detained the 12 migrants in a series of enforcement operations in Gua Musang, Pasir Mas and Kota Baru on June 23 and 28.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nearly 200,000 pick-up, jeep owners get extra Budi Diesel quota
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Nearly 200,000 owners of pick-up and jeep-type diesel vehicles nationwide have been approved for an additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel per month under the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) programme, the Finance Ministry (MOF) said.
In a statement today, MOF said the approval raises recipients' monthly subsidised diesel quota from 200 litres to 300 litres beginning this month, comprising more than 174,000 recipients in Peninsular Malaysia and over 22,000 in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan as of yesterday.
"This additional facility is provided taking into account the higher diesel consumption needs among pick-up and jeep owners, including for small and medium business use, as well as travel to rural and remote areas," it said.
Meanwhile, Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the additional allocation would help vehicle owners better manage their monthly diesel needs.
"The government understands that for diesel pick-up and jeep owners, these vehicles are not just for daily commuting, but are also used to earn a living and manage family needs," he told reporters after inspecting the implementation of the programme at a petrol station in Kota Kinabalu today.
Amir Hamzah said public response to Budi Diesel had been encouraging, with more than 238,000 users benefiting from it since its implementation in Peninsular Malaysia on June 27 and in Sabah and Sarawak on July 1.
He said the government had introduced a quota-transfer facility following feedback involving diesel vehicles used by immediate family members or whose ownership records had yet to be updated, allowing the subsidy to be utilised by the actual vehicle user rather than the registered owner.
For private diesel vehicles used by immediate family members who are not the registered owners, the original Budi Diesel recipient may apply to transfer eligibility through the Budi Madani portal, subject to the stipulated conditions and verification process.
Alternatively, actual vehicle users may update the ownership records with the Road Transport Department (RTD). To facilitate the process, the government has granted a three-month exemption on ownership transfer fees for diesel vehicles beginning July 1, 2026.
"This flexibility is given to ensure that those who are genuinely eligible and use the vehicle will benefit from the subsidy," he said, adding that more than 2,000 applications for the transfer of Budi Diesel eligibility had been received through the Budi Madani portal.
Owners of diesel pick-up and jeep vehicles may also apply for the additional 100-litre quota and transfer of eligibility through the portal at www.budimadani.gov.my.
He said more than 336,000 diesel transactions had been recorded between the start of Budi Diesel early access on June 27 and July 4, involving the purchase of 12.6 million litres of subsidised diesel and government subsidies amounting to more than RM23 million.
"These sales encompass the purchase of subsidised diesel at RM2.15 per litre during early access and RM2.10 per litre since the full implementation of Budi Diesel on July 1," he said.
On savings generated through the programme, Amir Hamzah said it was still too early to provide a precise figure as transaction data was still being collected and verified.
However, based on feedback from petrol station operators, diesel sales volumes had declined slightly, likely due to reduced purchases by those not eligible for the subsidy.
He expressed confidence that the programme would generate significant savings, enabling the government to channel more funds into assistance programmes for the people.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno unfazed after Puad speaks at PH event, says Ahmad Maslan
Article:
PONTIAN: Former Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi's attendance at a Pakatan Harapan event will not heavily impact the party, says Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan.
The Johor Umno deputy chief said Puad was no longer an Umno member and was free to attend PH functions.
"Umno members remain with the party and will not follow what Puad did after being disappointed that his child was not picked as a Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate," he said when met at Kampung Sungai Naga.
He said any potential impact would likely involve only a small number of fence-sitters rather than staunch Umno supporters.
Puad, who quit Umno last month, attended a PH programme at Felda Ulu Tebrau last night.
The former Johor state assembly speaker has also voiced support for PH's Rengit candidate, Mohamad Yazid Bakri.
During the Johor Youth Open Dialogue programme yesterday, Puad, the former Rengit assemblyman, was seen shaking hands and hugging PH chairman and Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Meanwhile, Ahmad, who is also an Umno Supreme Council member, said he did not expect such a large turnout at a recent BN rally held in Rengit.
"So, in my opinion, Puad can only influence a few people in Rengit, because what Puad did has violated the principles of the Umno struggle," he said.
The contest for Rengit will see a three-way fight between candidates from BN, PH, and Perikatan Nasional.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Full report on fatal Kem Hobart grenade blast expected by mid-month, says Khaled
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: The full investigation report on a grenade explosion that killed two soldiers at the Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah is expected to be submitted to the Defence Ministry by mid-month.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the ministry is awaiting the complete findings before deciding on further action.
"I understand that the report will be submitted by the middle of this month. God willing, I will report on it," he said after attending the Community Religious Education Program (PIKAT) at the Al-Faizin Jamek Mosque in Felda Air Tawar 2 here today.
He was commenting on the latest updates regarding the grenade explosion case in Gurun last month.
On whether existing standard operating procedures (SOPs) for training need to be reviewed, Khaled said the army has been directed to reassess its training approaches following two separate explosion incidents at the same camp.
"We have asked the army to evaluate the training approaches used, including the second incident that occurred at the same camp.
"We need to see if there are any other weaknesses and so on. We will only decide after receiving all the investigation findings," he said.
On June 16,
two soldiers
were killed in a grenade explosion during a training session at the Hobart Camp.
Police wrapped up their probe into the case before handing it over to the armed forces for further action and investigation.
Two weeks later,
another incident
occurred on June 29, injuring the legs of two soldiers during a shooting exercise at the same camp.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Cops probe video of assault on teen at JB bus station
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Police are investigating an assault on a teenager at a bus station here after a video of the incident went viral on social media.
The seven-second clip showed the teenager on the station floor while two men appeared to be attacking him, as a commuter attempted to break up the fight.
Johor Baru (South) police chief Assistant Commissioner Azrul Hisham Mohd Shaffei said a policeman lodged a report over the altercation at 4.30pm on Friday.
The incident had occurred earlier at 7.27am.
Police are investigating the case under Section 160 of the Penal Code for affray, which carries a jail term of up to six months, a fine of up to RM1,000, or both, upon conviction.
The motive behind the incident has yet to be established, while efforts are underway to identify and trace those involved, Azrul said in a statement today.
Members of the public with information can contact the district police hotline at 07-2182323.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 32-year-old says she's often mistaken for 80-year-old husband's daughter
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 48-year age gap often leads people to mistake 32-year-old Che Zainun Che Ahmad for the daughter of her 80-year-old husband, Hazlan Hajar.
Che Zainun said she was initially uncomfortable with the assumptions but has since grown accustomed to them.
She said she does not blame the public, as she had already prepared herself for various perceptions before deciding to marry a senior citizen.
"I married my husband 10 years ago after we got to know each other on Facebook over several years.
"At first, we were just friends, but his patience and maturity won my heart. I have also always preferred older men as partners," she said.
The Kelantan native said she had dated men her own age but found they were not compatible.
She said her family was initially shocked by the relationship but eventually gave their blessing.
"He was 70 when we married, while I was only 22," she said.
"To me, age is just a number. Thank God, we have been blessed with two children, aged nine years and one month."
Che Zainun said her husband maintains a healthy diet and remains in good health despite his age.
Although he is not a romantic person, she said his straightforward nature has helped keep their marriage peaceful and free of unnecessary conflict.
"With such a wide age gap, our interests and lifestyles are naturally different. I choose to compromise more for the sake of our marriage because older men tend to have strong egos," she said.
Despite her own experience, Che Zainun advised others against marrying someone with a significant age gap, particularly older divorcees or widowers.
"A relationship like this requires emotional maturity and resilience. Without that, the marriage may not last. Find someone who is mature, compatible and not too far apart in age," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Xi ready to work with Kim for 'stable' China-N. Korea ties
Article:
SEOUL: China's President Xi Jinping said he was ready to work with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to steer their bilateral relationship toward "long-term, sound and stable" development, Pyongyang's state media reported Sunday.
The exchange follows Xi's rare visit to North Korea last month, when the two leaders pledged to deepen ties as tensions rise on the Korean Peninsula and Pyongyang expands its military cooperation with Russia.
In a message dated July 1, Xi thanked Kim for his congratulatory message marking the 105th anniversary of the founding of China's Communist Party, according to the official Korean Central News Agency (KCNA).
Xi said Kim's message reflected the "deep and warm feelings" of the North Korean leader, the ruling Workers' Party and the North Korean people toward China.
"The Communist Party of China and the Workers' Party of Korea are the Marxist ruling parties," Xi said, adding that the two countries had stood together for national independence and had jointly advanced the socialist cause over generations.
"I am ready to guide the relevant sectors and regions of both sides to the full implementation of the important common understanding achieved among us and lead the China-DPRK relations to a long-term, sound and stable development," Xi said, using the initials of the North's official name.
Referring to his recent state visit to Pyongyang, Xi thanked Kim for the "enthusiastic and friendly" hospitality during the trip.
The message came in response to a letter from Kim, who described their summit in Pyongyang as a "historic occasion" and said it was North Korea's "steadfast stand" to continue strengthening ties with Beijing.
North Korea has expanded security ties with Russia, where Pyongyang has sent soldiers and munititions to assist Moscow's invasion of Ukraine.
Still, China remains North Korea's largest economic partner, accounting for nearly 98 per cent of the country's foreign trade in 2024, according to South Korea's Ministry of Economy and Finance.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China frees jailed pastor Ezra Jin
Article:
BEIJING: China has released pastor Ezra Jin, the founder of an underground church who has been detained since October, his church confirmed to AFP on Sunday.
Jin is the founder of Zion Church, one of China's unregistered churches that some Christians choose to worship at instead of state-sanctioned ones regulated by the government.
Jin, also known by his Chinese name Mingri, was detained on October 10 on "suspicion of the illegal use of information networks."
US President Donald Trump raised his case when he met his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping in May.
On Saturday, rights group ChinaAid said in a statement that Jin had arrived in Los Angeles after being released from detention in China.
The pastor was told by Chinese officials that his release "resulted from discussions between US President Donald J. Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, and was presented as a goodwill gesture coinciding with America's Independence Day", the statement said.
"We thank God for this tremendous miracle," Jin's daughter Grace said in a statement shared by a church member with AFP.
"We hope this is a signal of a positive turn for people of faith in China and relations between our two nations." --AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wedding trip ends as 2 men nabbed at roadblock with suspected ketum
Article:
KOTA BARU: Two men's plan to attend a wedding in Kelantan ended when they were detained at a roadblock in Lalang Pepuyu near Tanah Merah yesterday.
The men were detained by the General Operations Force (GOF) 9th Battalion under Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at about 10.30pm after a greenish liquid, suspected to be ketum juice, weighing about one litre and worth RM30, was found in the vehicle they were travelling in.
GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said officers on duty at the Lalang Pepuyu roadblock spotted a Perodua Aruz carrying two men behaving suspiciously.
He said the vehicle was ordered to stop, and a search led to the discovery of a black bag in the rear passenger footwell containing a green bottle filled with the suspected ketum liquid.
"They were detained and the seized items, along with the vehicle valued at RM50,265, were brought to the Komtak GOF at Taman Bakti for documentation purposes.
"The case has been referred to the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department of the Tanah Merah district police headquarters for further action under Section 30(3) of the Poisons Act 1952," he said in a statement.
He said the duo claimed they were heading to Kelantan to attend a wedding, and that the vehicle had been borrowed from the 29-year-old man's elder brother.
"Meanwhile, the 29-year-old said the ketum belonged to the other man, who is also his brother-in-law, and claimed he was unaware that the younger man was involved in ketum consumption.
"During questioning, the 20-year-old admitted the ketum was his and said he began consuming it earlier this year, obtaining supplies from a man near his home in Kuala Lumpur," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM: New digital system in the works for Johor-Singapore border easing
Article:
MUAR: A new digital system, to be launched jointly with Singapore in January next year, that is expected to ease congestion at the Johor-Singapore border.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, however, stopped short of revealing details of the initiative, saying doing so during the ongoing Johor election campaign could breach election regulations.
"I have the solution, but if I reveal it now, it could be against election rules," he said during the 'Jom Tanya PMX, Temu Anwar' and Bukit Naning volunteers launch held in Bakri here today.
He said traffic flow, border systems and congestion at the Johor-Singapore crossings had improved significantly over the past three years, adding that the new digital system would be launched together with Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong in January.
"Once the system is ready, InsyaAllah, travel from Singapore to Johor will be much faster," he added.
Responding to a participant's question on whether the government would improve border counter operations to reduce waiting times for Malaysians commuting daily to Singapore for work, Anwar said the government remained committed to addressing congestion through technological solutions.
"We are serious about resolving this issue. The use of advanced technology will help shorten the time people spend crossing the border," he said.
On Thursday, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said nearly
20 million QR code transactions
were recorded at Johor's two main land checkpoints since the rollout of the MyNIISe system.
He said the digital initiative had helped ease congestion at the Malaysia-Singapore border by encouraging the use of faster clearance lanes.
"As of June 28, MyNIISe had recorded 19.48 million QR code transactions at Bangunan Sultan Iskandar (BSI) and Kompleks Sultan Abu Bakar (KSAB), alongside 2.4 million downloads and 1.27 million users," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police: College skipped background check on employee in teen molest, sodomy case
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A college that hired a child sex offender, who is now suspected of molesting and sodomising two teenage students, did not conduct a background check before employing him.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said initial investigations found that the automotive college had failed to check whether the suspect had a criminal record.
"As a result, the college was unaware that the individual had a record involving sexual offences," he said when contacted.
Sarudin said police would record statements from the college's management to assist in the investigation.
He added that the suspect had been employed at the college for about two years.
So far, police have recorded statements from one of the complainants and the suspect's father.
"The suspect is currently remanded until July 10," he said, adding that police were also awaiting medical reports.
Yesterday, police announced the arrest of the man, who is suspected of sexually assaulting and sodomising two students.
The arrest followed a manhunt after police received reports from two 15-year-old boys.
The first complainant alleged that he was molested in a hostel toilet, while the second claimed he was sodomised at the suspect's house in Section 18 here.
The first case is being investigated under Section 14 of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, while the second is being investigated under Section 377C of the Penal Code for carnal intercourse against the order of nature.
Both offences carry a maximum jail term of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar-Onn Hafiz exchange just 'check and balance', not a quarrel, says Tok Mat
Article:
SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has dismissed suggestions that a public exchange between Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi amounted to a quarrel, describing it as a normal part of campaigning.
"That is just the ups and downs of campaigning, there is nothing to it, it is normal. I do not see it as a quarrel," said Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president.
Mohamad, popularly known as Tok Mat, said Anwar had merely rebuked Johor Menteri Besar Onn Hafiz, who then responded in kind, and that this back-and-forth was healthy rather than confrontational.
"There is no quarrel. In a campaign, sometimes it is like that. The PM rebukes the MB, the MB rebukes back — and that kind of mutual checking is good.
"That is what you call check and balance," he told reporters after officiating the 2026 Rembau Umno Division Youth, Wanita and Puteri delegates meeting here today.
On Friday, Anwar said it was inappropriate for Onn Hafiz to use the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) as campaign material for the Johor election.
Anwar said that the strategic project was a federal government initiative still going through the intergovernmental process between Malaysia and Singapore and should not be turned into political capital.
Yesterday, however, Onn Hafiz claimed that Anwar's statement on the
JS-SEZ masterplan document
was inaccurate and could mislead the public and investors.
Mohamad, who is also Foreign Minister and Rembau Umno chief, also debunked the narrative circulating in some quarters that voting for BN in the Johor election would help free former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak.
He said the outcome of the Johor election had absolutely nothing to do with Najib's situation.
"Datuk Seri Najib's position is a federal matter. We are a constitutional country with laws, and we must respect the law. It does not arise that if we win in Johor, Datuk Najib can be freed. That has absolutely nothing to do with it.
"So I hope Johor voters are not swayed or taken in by slander that if they vote BN, Datuk Najib will be freed. That is a separate matter entirely," he said.
Mohamad also said BN's campaign machinery was currently on very good momentum, but cautioned that it needed to be stepped up further given the attacks that were expected to intensify as polling day drew closer.
"BN's momentum so far is very good, but I hope the party machinery and their work are further strengthened because there are many attacks and we need to counter unreasonable attacks .
"There is a chance some people will fall for them.
"So explain things clearly, especially regarding the claim that if BN wins, Datuk Seri Najib will be freed," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Khaled Nordin says Hobart Camp blast investigation report will be submitted by mid‑month
Article:
KOTA TINGGI, July 5 — The investigation report into the grenade explosion that claimed the lives of two military personnel during a training exercise at Hobart Camp in Gurun last month is expected to be submitted to the Ministry of Defence by the middle of this month.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the report was being finalised before being submitted to the ministry for further action.
“I have been informed that the report will be submitted by the middle of this month. Insya-Allah, I will update you on it,” he told reporters after attending the ‘Ilmu dan Muafakat’ (PIKAT) programme at Felda Air Tawar 2 here today.
He said the Malaysian Army had also been directed to review its training approach, including taking into account the second incident that occurred at the same camp.
“We need to determine whether there were any other shortcomings or weaknesses. We will only make a decision after receiving the findings of the investigations,” he said.
In the incident at about 10.57am on June 16, the two personnel involved — Corporal Norazmi Abu Bakar of the Sixth Battalion, Royal Malay Regiment (RAMD), and Private Siti Khadijah Sungip of the First Squadron, Royal Engineers Regiment (RAJD) — sustained severe injuries in the explosion during the training exercise.
Both personnel were rushed to Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital in Sungai Petani for further treatment, but were pronounced dead while being transported to the hospital.
Two weeks later, on June 29, another incident occurred at the same camp when a firearm discharged during a combat enhancement training (LPT) exercise, injuring two military personnel.
Sergeant Mohamad Firdaus Che Shaharudin and Corporal Felix Franchis of the Fourth Battalion, Royal Ranger Regiment (4 RRD), suffered leg injuries after being struck by shrapnel in the 6.15 pm incident. — Bernama

Title: Defence Ministry wary of delay tactics as naval supplier seeks talks over cancelled RM1.06b deal, says minister
Article:
KOTA TINGGI, July 5 — The Defence Ministry has stated that the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) supplier has requested discussions over the RM1.06 billion claim stemming from the cancelled procurement for the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN).
However, Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the ministry is cautious about any discussion being used as an excuse or tactic to delay the claims process.
“The company has requested to meet with us. But while we are open to discussion, we do not want it to be used as a delaying tactic in the claims process.
“We are always aware and vigilant against any such tactics to avoid being trapped,”  he told reporters after attending the Ilmiah Keagamaan Masyarakat (PIKAT) programme at Felda Air Tawar 2 today.
The Norwegian government was previously reported to have revoked the export licence for the NSM anti-ship missiles and their launch systems on security grounds.
According to Kongsberg Defence and Aerospace’s (KDA) website, the NSM missile deal was signed with RMN in April 2018 at EUR124 million (RM571.9 million) to equip six new Littoral Combat Ships (LCS).
Following the cancellation, Malaysia is claiming RM1.06 billion from KDA, comprising EUR129.86 million (RM604 million) in direct claims for payments already made, plus EUR96.26 million (RM448.12 million) in indirect claims covering cost implications and knock-on effects from the failed supply.
In a related development, Mohamed Khaled said an evaluation team of navy officers has been formed to identify the best missile system from four countries to replace KDA’s Norwegian-made NSM.
“Yes, it’s underway, and we want it evaluated ASAP,” he said briefly.
On June 28, Mohamed Khaled was reported as saying the ministry had identified four potential countries to supply the anti-ship missile system for the LCS following Norway’s cancellation. Turkey and South Korea are among the contenders, along with two European nations.
He said the selection process goes beyond basic specifications, factoring in key criteria to ensure the chosen system meets the navy’s operational needs. — Bernama

Title: From shopping carts to prayer mats: Minister wants Friday prayers expanded to malls nationwide
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM, July 5 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan has welcomed states across the country to hold Friday prayers at shopping malls, following the positive response to the initiative at several locations in the Federal Territories.
Zulkifli said the initiative was first introduced in the Federal Territories through a collaboration between the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (Jawi) and the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Council (MAIWP), as it falls under the jurisdiction of the two agencies.
“So far, Friday prayers have been held at several shopping malls, including IOI City Mall, Alamanda Shopping Centre and Suria KLCC, while discussions are underway to expand the initiative to several other locations.
“As for the states, we encourage and welcome any state to join us. Insya-Allah, I believe this good initiative will receive a positive response,” he told reporters after officiating the national-level Multaqa Madani Ulama Umara and the launch of the Madani Mushaf at Masjid Timah in Permatang Pauh here today.
Malaysia currently has more than 6,784 registered mosques, over 1,029 registered Friday surau, and more than 18,000 surau nationwide, served by a total of 11,000 imams who receive monthly allowances.
Zulkifli said he also received enquiries from the management of several shopping malls interested in establishing a Friday surau after seeing the encouraging response to the initiative.
He said malls that introduced such facilities had become more vibrant and attracted higher visitor numbers.
He added that several states had also expressed interest in the initiative, particularly those with major urban centres, rapid development and high-rise commercial buildings, although discussions were still at an early stage.
“That is why more shopping mall managements are approaching us to explore the possibility of implementing the initiative at their premises. Beyond shopping malls, we also plan to expand Friday prayer facilities to other public locations such as transportation terminals, airports and highway rest and service areas, to make it easier for Muslims to perform Friday prayers,” he said.
Meanwhile, Zulkifli said the Rakan Muda Rakan Masjid programme would be expanded nationwide to encourage greater youth participation in enlivening mosques through a range of religious, community and recreational activities.
He said the programme, launched by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, is being rolled out in stages across the country, including in Penang. The initiative aims to bring young people closer to mosques through activities that match their interests, such as sports, recreational pursuits and social programmes.
“In the Federal Territories, we have brought together mosque youth committees from more than 75 mosques, which are now actively organising various programmes for the community. The government will also continue to strengthen the role of mosques nationwide as part of its efforts to enhance the administration of Islamic affairs,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Labourer in Kelantan gets four years’ jail, RM6,000 fine for abducting man over brother’s debt and stealing Volkswagen Jetta
Article:
PASIR MAS, July 5 — A labourer was sentenced to four years’ jail and fined RM6,000 by the Sessions Court here today on two charges of kidnapping and car theft in Tendong last month.
Judge Zulkpli Abdullah handed down the sentence after Mohamad Syamsul Azroy Che Anuar, 32, pleaded guilty to the charges, and ordered an additional six months’ imprisonment if he fails to pay the fine.
On the first charge, Mohamad Syamsul and three others still at large were accused of kidnapping Mohd Shahrul Nizam Mohd Asri, 35, with the intent to secretly and wrongfully confine him at a house in Tendong at about 3.30pm on June 6.
He was charged under Section 365 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same Code, which carries a penalty of up to seven years’ imprisonment and a fine.
The court sentenced the accused to four years’ jail from the date of his arrest on June 16, along with a RM3,000 fine for the kidnapping offence, with an additional three months’ jail if the fine is not paid.
On the second charge, he and the same three individuals still at large were accused of stealing a car belonging to Mohd Taufik Mohd Asri, 25, at the same location, time and date.
He was charged under Section 379A(1) of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same Code, which carries a penalty of up to seven years’ imprisonment and a fine.
However, the court sentenced him to three years’ jail and a RM3,000 fine, with an additional three months’ imprisonment if the fine is unpaid. Both sentences are to run concurrently.
Deputy public prosecutor Siti Aisyha Na’ilah Harizan appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.
According to the facts of the case, Mohd Shahrul Nizam was kidnapped on June 6 after four individuals, including the accused, approached him looking for the victim’s brother, Mohd Taufik, over an unpaid debt.
The victim was abducted and taken to the border via an illegal route before being rescued by Thai police the following day. The group also stole a Volkswagen Jetta. — Bernama

Title: Move over Musang King because Mardi is prepping three top-secret premium durian hybrids
Article:
KUALA KANGSAR, July 5 — The Malaysian Agricultural Research and Development Institute (Mardi) has planted about 25,000 trees of the MRDI Super 88 (MS88) hybrid durian nationwide to date.
Its director-general Datuk Dr Mohamad Zabawi Abdul Ghani said the planting covers about 242 hectares, mainly in Selangor and the east coast states.
“The MS88 is one of three premium durian varieties developed by Mardi since the early 2000s, together with MDUR78 and MDUR79, which remain popular among durian enthusiasts,” he told reporters after the Duriotourism Mardi Kuala Kangsar programme with the media at the Mardi Agrotechnology Park here today.
In another development, Mohamad Zabawi said Mardi is selecting three new hybrid durian varieties with potential to be introduced as premium types to meet industry demand and consumer preference.
He said the varieties are currently being evaluated for fruit quality, consumer acceptance and suitability for cultivation by farmers.
“So far, we have several hybrids identified for future release, but they are still under selection. We want to ensure they are well received by consumers and suitable for farmers,” he said.
He added that the names of the varieties cannot yet be disclosed as they are planned for launch during the durian season, allowing the public to view and taste them. — Bernama

Title: Mohamad Hasan hints BN could shake up ‘traditional seats’ for Negeri Sembilan polls
Article:
SEREMBAN, July 5 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is likely to make changes by no longer retaining “traditional seats” in the division of constituencies contested by the coalition in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election.
BN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said the move was due to the composition of voters in most constituencies having changed significantly.
“We want to look again and, if possible, we want to modify it so that it gives component parties in the BN an opportunity to contest in constituencies where they feel they can win. Compared to the traditional way, for example, seats 1, 2, 3, 4 — these are yours.
“So, it does not provide space and also does not give voters the opportunity to choose. That is my view as the Negeri Sembilan BN chairman: do a seat swap because we must give voters an opportunity.
“If it is getting worse, why do we want to give the same party to contest in the same place? We may change seats, but we will try to accommodate as many friends as possible among BN components,” he said after officiating the Rembau Division Wanita, Youth and Puteri Umno delegates’ meeting here today.
Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president, said previous election results would be used as a guide in assessing voter support patterns and demographic changes in each area before any decision was made.
However, he said any final decision regarding the distribution of seats and the list of candidates for component parties, including BN’s allies, was subject to the decision of the BN Supreme Council at the central level.
He said all division heads had been instructed to submit their respective lists of candidates according to the established procedure, with at least three candidates for each seat.
“We will do it immediately because we want to announce the names of the candidates on July 15 when we launch the BN election machinery. We don’t have much time left,” he said.
Mohamad said BN cannot afford any internal disputes ahead of the election because past experience has shown that several seats were lost due to sabotage by its own members.
Meanwhile, he did not rule out the possibility of defending the Rantau state seat, which he has held since 2004, but said the matter would depend on the party leadership’s decision.
The Election Commission (EC) has set polling day for the Negeri Sembilan state election on August 1, with nomination on July 18 and early voting on July 28. — Bernama

Title: Fahmi: Johor among priorities as MCMC moves to improve internet coverage with shared networks
Article:
BATU PAHAT, July 5 — The Communications Ministry, through the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), plans to expand the implementation of the Multi-Operator Core Network (MOCN) nationwide to improve connectivity in areas with limited internet coverage.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the initiative would enable telecommunications companies to collaborate, particularly in extending coverage to rural, remote and underserved areas with weak signal reception.
He said that under the MOCN initiative, telecommunications companies with existing infrastructure in a particular area could share their network with other operators, allowing mobile signals to be transmitted and received more comprehensively.
“At present, the MOCN initiative has been implemented at five locations — two telecommunications towers at Bukit Putus in Negeri Sembilan, and one each at Prima Gambang in Pahang, the Kota Seri Langat Toll Plaza on the West Coast Expressway (WCE) in Banting, Selangor, and Tanjung Asam in Penang.
“In Johor, we are currently identifying several areas for implementation. Based on assessments and studies carried out by the MCMC, areas with limited 4G or 5G coverage are mainly located in rural parts of the state,” he told reporters after the Communications Ministry’s Ziarah Kasih programme at Kampung Parit Maimon here today.
Fahmi said one of the main factors contributing to poor mobile signal reception was that many homes were located in villages and rural areas surrounded or shielded by oil palm trees.
“Johor remains a priority for the ministry and the MCMC as we work towards achieving 100 per cent internet coverage in populated areas,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Police: Shah Alam college failed to check employee’s criminal record before hiring him in teen molest, sodomy case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — Police say an automotive college that hired a man now suspected of molesting and sodomising two teenage students did not carry out a background check before employing him.
According to the
New Straits Times
, initial investigations found the college had failed to verify whether the employee had a criminal record.
“As a result, the college was unaware that the individual had a record involving sexual offences,” district police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said.
Sarudin said police will record statements from the college’s management to assist the investigation.
He said the suspect had worked at the college for about two years.
Police have so far recorded statements from one of the complainants and the suspect’s father.
Sarudin said the suspect is currently remanded until July 11 and that police are awaiting medical reports.
Police arrested the man yesterday after receiving reports from two 15‑year‑old boys.
The first complainant alleged he was molested in a hostel toilet, while the second claimed he was sodomised at the suspect’s house in Section 18, Shah Alam.
Police said the first case is being investigated under Section 14 of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, while the second is being investigated under Section 377C of the Penal Code for carnal intercourse against the order of nature.
Both offences carry a maximum jail term of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
